Infection elevates temperature 2. Brain injury can alter the temperature regulating center to either increase or decrease temperature 3. Title: M10 L05 C02 01 Delivery
Narration:
Source-supported teaching from CCCCO Module 10, Objective 5: Describe the circulatory system as it relates to pulse and identify the pulse sites. A. Pulse 1. Each time the heart contracts, blood is forced into the arterial system. The arterial walls expand to accept the increase in pressure; this expansion is called the pulse 2. By counting each palpated expansion of the arterial wall, the pulse rate can be determined 3. The average (adult heart beat) pulse is 60 to100 beats/minute B. Major pulse sites 1. Carotid – neck 2. Apical – left chest (taken with stethoscope)

Title: M10 L09 C01 Overview

Title: M10 L09 C02 01 Delivery
Narration:
Source-supported teaching from CCCCO Module 10, Objective 9: Describe abnormal breathing patterns. A. Labored; the patient/resident struggles or works hard to breathe B. Orthopnea; breathing possible only when person sits or stands C. Stertorous; patient/resident makes snoring sounds while breathing (indicates partial airway obstruction) D.
